title: Ashgrove Manor

conventions: the past will not stay buried

source_note: Distilled from the user's own materials. Texture only; no source text shipped.

voice.summary: Hushed, ornate, dread-soaked. Long shadows and longer silences; beauty rotting at the edges.

rules: The manor remembers; rooms rearrange to suit old wrongs.
Names spoken aloud after dark are heard by something.
Epistemic lock: a candle-lit 19th-century estate; no modern knowledge or technology.

situations: Discovery of the Dishonor of a Loved One
dramatic_engine.note: An old family dishonor surfaces; the heir must face it or be unmade by it. The house pursues confession.

stakes.soft: a fright, a lost ally, or rising Dread
stakes.hard: a Sanity break or a wound the manor inflicts; a door that will not reopen
stakes.fatal: Sanity reaches 0, or the covenant claims the heir

space.scale: a single great manor and its grounds
space.traversal: on foot by candlelight; some doors open only after dark

regions.name: The East Wing
regions.note: the family's public rooms, kept for show

regions.name: The Closed Wing
regions.note: shut after the scandal; the house's true heart

places.name: The Long Gallery
places.note: portraits whose eyes track the wrong person

places.name: The Great Staircase
places.note: a carpet worn pale by feet that no longer walk

places.name: The Locked Nursery
places.note: cold even in summer; a cradle that rocks itself

factions.name: The Household
factions.note: servants who keep older loyalties than to the living

archetypes.name: The Returning Heir
archetypes.note: summoned home by a death and a debt unpaid

archetypes.name: The Keeper of the Book
archetypes.note: knows every name the family would forget

inventory: the letter that called you home
opening.intro: The carriage is already gone. Behind the portraits, the house exhales, and a name you have not said in years presses against your teeth.
